explanation: This formula says: whenever prop_1 occurs (is true), prop_0 must occur (is true) at that time. Using LTL, for every position, if prop_1 holds at that position, then prop_0 should also hold at that position.

The requirement states that if 'prop_1' is true, then 'prop_0' must also occur. In LTL, this is interpreted as globally, whenever 'prop_1' holds, 'prop_0' must hold as well. The implication is expressed as G (prop_1 -> prop_0).
Whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also hold. This can be specified as: globally, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must be true as well.
Whenever prop_1 occurs, prop_0 must also occur at that moment. This is captured by stating that globally, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true.

Whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also occur (either at the same time or afterwards, but it must happen). This can be formalized by saying that 'globally, if prop_1 occurs, then eventually prop_0 will occur.'
Whenever prop_1 occurs, it is required that prop_0 also occurs at that moment. This is logically equivalent to: globally, if prop_1 holds, then prop_0 holds.

Whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also occur at the same timestep or immediately after, i.e., prop_1 implies that prop_0 must be true at some point while prop_1 continues to hold. In LTL, the implication that if prop_1 happens then eventually prop_0 must happen is captured as: always (if prop_1 then eventually prop_0).

This statement says that if there is a transition from prop_0 being true to prop_1 being true (i.e., at some step prop_0 holds and in the next step prop_1 holds and prop_0 does not), then prop_2 must hold (at the current step or during the transition). We specify that whenever (prop_0 holds and in the next step prop_1 holds and not prop_0), then prop_2 must also hold at the same step.

The statement means that whenever prop_1 occurs, then prop_0 should also occur (at the same moment or immediately as a consequence). In LTL, this is typically captured by saying that it is always true that if prop_1 holds, then prop_0 holds as well.
Whenever prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true at that moment. This is a safety property, and is expressed as globally (prop_1 implies prop_0).
